tokenization.title: Tokenization
tokenization.description: Your text is split into tokens, the basic units the model reads. Usually one word per token, but punctuation and rare words can split further.

tokenID.description: EACH TOKEN IS GIVEN A UNIQUE ID THAT CORRESPONDS TO A ROW IN THE EMBEDDING MATRIX
tokenID.matrixLabel: These IDs index into the embedding matrix
tokenID.matrixSlice: Embedding Matrix (100k × 768) — showing a tiny slice
tokenID.title: Token IDs
tokenID.description2: Each token maps to an integer index in the vocabulary (~100k tokens for modern LLMs). The model never sees raw text, only these numbers.
tokenID.embeddingNote: These IDs are used to select a row in the embedding matrix.

embedding.instruction: CLICK A TOKEN TO INSPECT ITS EMBEDDING VECTOR
embedding.title: Embeddings
embedding.description: A learned matrix converts each ID into a dense vector. Similar words end up with similar vectors.

qkv.instruction: CLICK A TOKEN TO SEE HOW ITS EMBEDDING PRODUCES Q, K, V
qkv.title: Embedding → QKV Projection
qkv.description: The attention layer converts each embedding vector into three different representations.
qkv.note: The same embedding vector is projected into three different spaces using separate learned weight matrices.

selfAttention.instruction: CLICK A TOKEN TO SEE WHICH OTHER TOKENS IT PAYS ATTENTION TO
selfAttention.head: Head {head} / 12
selfAttention.error: Failed to fetch attention data. Is the backend running?
selfAttention.title: Self-Attention
selfAttention.description1: The Query vector for the selected token is compared with the Key vectors of every token using a dot product. The resulting scores are normalized with softmax to produce attention weights.
selfAttention.description2: Attention weights show how important each token is when processing the selected token. Higher weights mean the model focuses more on that token.

attentionOut.instruction: APPLY ATTENTION TO PRODUCE OUTPUT (SHOWING 4 / 64 DIMENSIONS)
attentionOut.title: Attention Output
attentionOut.description1: Each token attends to previous tokens using a row of the attention matrix.
attentionOut.description2: These weights scale the value vectors and are summed to form the output.

mlp.title: MLP + Residual
mlp.description1: The MLP transforms the attention output by learning more complex patterns and relationships.
mlp.description2: A residual connection then adds the original input back to preserve important information and stabilize learning.

output.title: Next Token Prediction
output.description1: The model uses the final representation of the last token to predict what word comes next.
output.description2: Click any word to see how the sentence would continue with that prediction.
